entries.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between relief, climate and natural vegetation
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe the major climatic regions of the world (tropical, temperate, polar and sub-tropical)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on major world climatic regions
Group Discussion: Guide students to characterise the major world climatic regions
Field visit: Guide students to observe local climate characteristics and associate them with the world climatic regions
Graphical and Maps presentation: Guide students to draw graphs and maps to characterise major world climatic regions
entries.assessment: The major climatic regions of the World are categorised or classified clearly
resources: Map, graphs, photographs, globe, variety of living organisms, and online materials on the major climatic regions of the world.
entries.references: TIE, Geography for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between relief, climate and natural vegetation
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe the relationship between major features of the Earth’s surface, climate, and natural vegetation
teaching_learning_activities: Question & Answers: Guide students to describe the relationship between major features of the Earth’s surface, climate and natural vegetation
Case study: Guide students to justify the relationship between major features of the Earth’s surface, climate and natural vegetation
entries.assessment: The relationship between major features of the Earth’s surface, climate and natural vegetation are correctly described
resources: Maps, photographs, globe and online materials on the relationship between major features of the earth’s surface, climate and natural vegetations
entries.references: TIE, Geography for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0  Demonstrate mastery of basic skills and techniques in Geography
entries.specific_competence: 2.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the basic techniques in land surveying
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explore the basic tenets of surveying (meaning, types, equipment, methods, and importance)
teaching_learning_activities: Group Discussion: Guide students to explain the basic concepts of surveying
Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on methods and importance of surveying
Guest speaker: Invite resourceful persons from surveying institutions/ companies to share experiences on surveying activities
entries.assessment: The basic tenets of surveying are correctly explored
resources: Relevant books on land surveying, tape measure/chain, Arrows, plumb bob, cross-staff ranging poles, pegs, notebook, pencil/pen, and relevant ICT tools
entries.references: TIE, Geography for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0  Demonstrate mastery of basic skills and techniques in Geography
entries.specific_competence: 2.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the basic techniques in land surveying
entries.learning_activity: (b) Carry out simple tape/chain surveying
teaching_learning_activities: Project work: Guide students to carry out simple surveying activities around the schools and their local communities
Guest speaker: Invite resourceful persons from surveying institutions/ companies to share experiences and conduct simple surveying activities
Group Discussion: Guide students to discuss and apply the findings from surveying on socio-economic and environmental activities
entries.assessment: The simple tape/ chain surveying is correctly carried out
resources: Relevant books on land surveying, tape measure/chain, Arrows, plumb bob, cross-staff ranging poles, pegs, notebook, pencil/pen, and relevant ICT tools
entries.references: TIE, Geography for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of human geography
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Carry out a project in Geography
entries.learning_activity: (a) Develop and carry out a simple research project in Geography
teaching_learning_activities: Project work: Guide students in groups to prepare a good research title for the project
Field work: Guide students to conduct a simple research project in the field on geographical research titles of their choice.
entries.assessment: The research title of each group is correctly prepared. A simple research project is effectively conducted in the field by each group
resources: Environment, institutions, note books, pen, pencil, key informants, camera/smart-phones, and relevant ICT tools
entries.references: TIE, Geography for Secondary Schools Form Three: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of human geography
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Carry out a project in Geography
entries.learning_activity: (a) Develop and carry out a simple research project in Geography
teaching_learning_activities: Project work: Guide students in groups to prepare a good research title for the project
Field work: Guide students to conduct a simple research project in the field on geographical research titles of their choice.
entries.assessment: The research title of each group is correctly prepared. A simple research project is effectively conducted in the field by each group
